title: Argentinian Creamy Potato Hash
description: A flavorful Argentinean dish made with small potatoes, creamy cheese, and crispy onions. Perfect for meal prep and a great side dish.
instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of butter in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the sliced onion and cook until it's translucent and slightly caramelized, about 10-12 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add the diced potatoes to the skillet and stir to combine with the onion and garlic mixture.
Cook the potatoes for about 5-7 minutes, until they start to brown.
Stir in the shredded gruyere cheese and cook until it's melted and the potatoes are coated in the cheese sauce.
Remove the skillet from the heat and stir in the heavy cream.
Season the potato mixture with salt and black pepper to taste.
Transfer the potato mixture to a serving dish and garnish with chopped fresh parsley.
Serve the potato hash hot and enjoy!
